plainEnglish: At Level AAA, blocks of text must let users control presentation for readability: selectable foreground/background colors, lines no wider than 80 characters, no full justification, line spacing of at least 1.5 within paragraphs (with larger gaps between paragraphs), and resizing to 200% without horizontal scrolling. Provide these defaults or a mechanism to set them.
whyItMatters: People with dyslexia and low vision lose their place in text that is fully justified, too wide, or tightly spaced, and need control over these presentation settings.
quickCheck: Check body-text blocks: max width around 80 characters, line-height at least 1.5, not justified, and readable at 200% zoom without horizontal scrolling.
commonMistakes: Using text-align: justify on body copy, creating uneven 'rivers' of whitespace.
Full-width lines that far exceed 80 characters because there's no max-width.
Cramped line-height below 1.5 in long-form content.

codeExamples.label: Readable text block: left-aligned, ~80 chars, 1.5 line-height
Justified, full-width, tightly-spaced text
